memories.memory_text: I remember asking Tracy if she wanted to share a dessert, and then saying 'one, two, three' like we used to do—only she said 'I want a divorce' while I said 'crème brûlée.'
grounded_facts: Cal suggested sharing a dessert after finishing bread.
Cal initiated a game of saying their choices at the same time with a count of three.
Tracy responded with 'I want a divorce' as Cal said 'crème brûlée'.

memories.memory_text: I had the takeout box in my lap, and she asked me if I was going to say anything—I wanted to speak, I turned toward her, but I just... couldn't. I looked away and stayed silent.
grounded_facts: Cal is sitting in the passenger seat of the SUV with a to-go box from the French restaurant in his lap.
Tracy asks Cal twice if he is going to say anything.
Cal begins to turn toward Tracy as if to speak, but stops himself and looks forward again.
The scene ends in silence between them.

memories.memory_text: I remember the moment she said it—David Jacobowitz, from work—and all I could think was, please stop, just stop talking before I do something irreversible.
grounded_facts: Cal speaks for the first time after Tracy reveals the affair
saying
Please stop.

memories.memory_text: I opened the door while the car was still moving and stepped out—I don’t even know how I landed—because her voice kept going and I couldn’t feel anything else.
grounded_facts: Cal opens the car door and steps out of the moving vehicle after saying
If you keep talking, I'm going to get out of the car.

memories.memory_text: I remember standing there with blood on my face, saying I’d leave that night—just stop talking about it, Tracy, please—like if she kept going, I’d break completely.
grounded_facts: Cal is physically injured with blood on his forehead.
Cal verbally commits to leaving the marriage and signing papers.
Tracy touches his forehead, showing lingering affection.
Cal limps back to the car after the exchange.

memories.memory_text: I told Robbie I jumped out of a moving car after Tracy told me she wanted a divorce—then saw the look on his face when he realized it was true.
grounded_facts: Cal reveals that Tracy told him she wants a divorce.
Cal states he jumped out of a moving car in response.
Robbie reacts with shock and asks Cal about the divorce and the car.
Cal apologizes to Robbie for how he found out.
Jessica notices Cal is bleeding before he speaks.

memories.memory_text: I was driving, still numb, and I kept repeating to myself, 'We'll talk to the kids tomorrow. Just separating. We'll do it together.' I even thought about Nanna—how crushed she'd be—but decided not to tell her yet. She’s got enough on her plate.
grounded_facts: Cal says he will talk to the kids tomorrow about the separation.
Cal considers telling his mother (Nanna) but decides against it for now, believing she will be crushed.
The decision to delay telling Nanna is based on her having 'enough to worry about.'

memories.memory_text: I was trying to act normal with Jessica, asking about Stanford, when she pointed out I was bleeding. Then it hit me—David Jacobowitz—and I just lost it, pounding the steering wheel, screaming his name. I couldn’t stop myself.
grounded_facts: Cal suddenly erupts in anger, banging the steering wheel and shouting 'David Jacobowitz!' and 'GOD DAMN HER!'.
The outburst occurs after Jessica mentions Cal is bleeding and before he pulls up to the driveway.
Cal is interrupted mid-rage and eventually calms down.

memories.memory_text: I broke down in the bathroom at work, and now my boss thinks it's a relief that I'm just getting a divorce instead of dying. I had to leave, said I needed to buy furniture—anything to get out of there before I said something worse.
grounded_facts: Cal was crying in the bathroom at work, which coworkers noticed.
His boss misinterpreted the reason for his distress, initially fearing illness.
Cal revealed his divorce in response to his boss's praise.
Cal abruptly left work, citing the need to buy furniture as a reason to exit.

memories.memory_text: I told the salesman my wife is sleeping with David Jacobowitz—just blurted it out, like the words had been stuck in my throat for months and finally tore free.
grounded_facts: Cal says 'My wife is sleeping with David Jacobowitz' to the salesman unprompted.
The statement is delivered immediately upon the salesman's approach, without prior discussion of personal matters.

memories.memory_text: I remember saying 'I want it in beige' after he patted the chaise, the fabric swatch rough under my fingers, like I was claiming something real for the first time in years.
grounded_facts: Cal explicitly states 'I want it in beige' after the salesman suggests the chaise and pats it.
The choice of beige is made following a moment of processing, indicating a deliberate decision.

memories.memory_text: I was sitting at the bar, drained, watching Jacob already with another woman at my usual table, and when some guy tried to take the seat next to me, I snapped — yelled something ugly about my wife screwing David Jacobowitz, loud enough for everyone to hear.
grounded_facts: Cal is at the bar, looking exhausted.
Cal sees Jacob at his usual table, now with a new woman.
A man attempts to sit on the open stool next to Cal.
Cal tells the man he's waiting for a friend.
The man points out Cal has been holding the seat for an hour.
Cal responds loudly, revealing his wife is cheating with David Jacobowitz.
The man shrugs and leaves.
Cal checks his watch, places a napkin on his drink, and stands up.

memories.memory_text: Bernie showed up with a wrapped gift, handed it to me, and said it was cologne—like it was some kind of farewell present—then told me Claire forced him to end our friendship.
grounded_facts: Bernie arrives late at the bar with a shopping bag.
Bernie gives Cal a wrapped package from Macy's.
Bernie says the gift is cologne.
Bernie states that Claire won't let him be friends with Cal anymore.

memories.memory_text: After Bernie left, I sat there staring at my drink, and then I saw this guy—Jacob—watching me with this look like I was a complete mess, like I was something broken he could fix.
grounded_facts: Cal is left alone at the bar after Bernie exits.
Jacob is observed watching Cal from a distance.
Jacob reacts to Cal's appearance with disapproval.
Jacob dismisses the woman at his table after observing Cal.

memories.scene_title: 19、EXT. THE WEAVER BACKYARD - DAY (ONE WEEK LATER)
memories.memory_text: I told Tracy to make sure my azaleas got enough sun—anything to avoid saying what I really felt—then got in the truck, and backed straight into the fence.
grounded_facts: Cal said 'Make sure my azaleas get enough sun' to Tracy before leaving.
Cal backed the U-Haul into the neighboring fence immediately after starting to drive away.

memories.scene_title: 19、EXT. THE WEAVER BACKYARD - DAY (ONE WEEK LATER)
memories.memory_text: She asked me when we stopped being 'us,' and I looked at her and said it was when she screwed David Jacobowitz—then I saw her flinch, and I didn’t take it back.
grounded_facts: Tracy asked Cal when they stopped being 'us'.
Cal responded by accusing Tracy of cheating with David Jacobowitz.
Tracy's face showed pain, but she nodded in understanding after Cal's statement.

memories.memory_text: Molly looked like she was about to cry, and then she asked me, 'Don't you love us anymore?' I took her face in my hands, my voice breaking as I tried to tell her this wasn't their fault.
grounded_facts: Molly appeared upset and asked if Cal still loved them.
Cal responded by holding Molly's face and beginning to tear up.
Cal told the children that what was happening with their parents was not their fault.

memories.memory_text: Robbie took a breath, asked if he could say something with a curse, and then just said it: 'Love is fucked.' I laughed, but it hit me hard—my kid shouldn't feel that way.
grounded_facts: Robbie asked for permission to use a curse word.
Robbie stated 'Love is fucked' in a serious tone.
Cal responded with a laugh, though visibly affected.

memories.memory_text: I bent down and took off one sneaker, then the other, and handed them over without a word—just shrugged like it meant nothing, even though they were my favorite pair.
grounded_facts: Cal removes both sneakers after being instructed by Jacob.
Cal hands the shoes to Jacob after a shrug.
The sneakers were personally significant to Cal, described by him as his favorite.

memories.memory_text: He threw them over the railing, just like that—and then screamed at me, 'ARE YOU IN A FRATERNITY, CAL!?' like I’d committed some crime just by wearing the wrong damn shoes.
grounded_facts: Jacob throws Cal's sneakers over the railing of the food court.
Jacob confronts Cal with the question 'ARE YOU IN A FRATERNITY, CAL!?' in a loud, aggressive tone.
Cal reacts with shock and disbelief to the destruction of his shoes.

memories.memory_text: I stepped out of the dressing room feeling ridiculous but also... good, really good — and then Tiffany said 'Wow,' and for a second I believed it.
grounded_facts: Cal completed a physical transformation with new clothes and haircut.
Tiffany reacted with 'Wow' upon seeing Cal emerge.

memories.memory_text: Jacob didn't just mock how I talk — he told me my wife cheated because I failed her as a husband, as a man, and something in me just... broke.
grounded_facts: Jacob delivered a cruel psychological attack linking Cal's personality to his wife's infidelity.
Cal reacted with visible emotional distress, lip quivering, after Jacob's comment.

memories.memory_text: I told Jacob I've only ever been with one woman—Emily—and when I said I still miss her, he just told me to shut up like it was nothing.
grounded_facts: Cal reveals he has been sexually intimate with only one person in his life, his wife Emily Weaver.
Cal expresses that he misses his wife, showing emotional vulnerability.
Jacob dismisses Cal's emotional statement with 'Shut up, Cal.'

memories.memory_text: I froze when Jacob used my exact words about someone being wildly sexy and incredibly cute—those were the words I used for Emily, and he just stole them like they meant nothing.
grounded_facts: Cal recalls describing his wife as 'wildly sexy and incredibly cute,' a personal and meaningful phrase to him.
Jacob later uses the same phrase to seduce another woman, which Cal witnesses.
Cal reacts with visible shock and horror, realizing his private sentiment has been copied.

memories.memory_text: I remember kissing Kate and blurting out that I’d never been with anyone but my ex—then panicking and joking about her having AIDS, which somehow made her want me more.
grounded_facts: Cal and Kate passionately kiss upon entering the apartment.
Cal admits he's never been with another woman besides his ex-wife.
Cal says he's worried Kate has AIDS, then clarifies 'only a little,' which leads to her becoming more aroused.

memories.memory_text: I walked into the bar feeling lighter, like I could finally own this new version of myself—joking with Jacob about stupid ideas like a candy store with only red candy, and actually laughing like I meant it.
grounded_facts: Cal strides into the bar with confidence, in a tailored suit, everything in slow motion.
Cal is seen winking at a waitress and being observed with envy.
Cal debates with Jacob about absurd business ideas, including a red-only candy store.
Cal insists no one likes lemon or grape, while Jacob counters that he likes grape, leading to shared laughter.

memories.scene_title: 41、EXT. CAL'S CORPORATE APARTMENT - LATER THAT DAY
memories.memory_text: Robbie asked me if I still love his mom, and I couldn’t answer. I just stood there, silent, while the weight of it pressed down on me.
grounded_facts: Robbie directly asked Cal, 'Do you still love Mom?'
Cal did not respond verbally; his silence was noted as significant in the scene
The moment occurred during a catch session between Cal and Robbie outside Cal's apartment

memories.scene_title: 41、EXT. CAL'S CORPORATE APARTMENT - LATER THAT DAY
memories.memory_text: Robbie threw the ball hard, yelling for me to fight for love, to get his mom back — and I didn’t even catch it. It went right through a car window while I was still stuck in my head.
grounded_facts: Robbie delivered a passionate speech urging Cal to win back his ex-wife
Robbie punctuated his words by throwing the ball forcefully toward Cal
Cal failed to catch the ball, which broke the window of a nearby car
Cal was described as 'lost in thought' during the moment

memories.memory_text: I said 'We have a mutual friend' and tried to steer it back to Robbie, but Kate kept circling, writing those words on the board like she was carving them into me.
grounded_facts: Cal attempted to downplay his connection to Kate by saying they had a mutual friend.
He tried to redirect the conversation to Robbie's academic performance.
Kate wrote 'ASSHOLE' on the board and linked it to Cal's past behavior.

memories.memory_text: I shouted 'OKAY! ENOUGH!!!' and turned to Tracy saying 'I can explain,' but she was already getting up — I saw her face, she believed it, and then she ran.
grounded_facts: Cal interrupted Kate by shouting 'OKAY! ENOUGH!!!'.
He addressed Tracy directly with 'I can explain'.
Tracy appeared crestfallen and left the room immediately after.

memories.memory_text: I told Tracy I loved her, right there in front of everyone, and when I said I'd slept with our son's teacher, the whole crowd gasped like I was some monster — and then I made it worse, stumbling over my words, trying to say she was still the only one I wanted, but it just came out wrong.
grounded_facts: Cal publicly confesses his love to Tracy at the school.
He admits to sleeping with their son's eighth-grade teacher in front of a crowd.
He attempts to redeem himself by declaring Tracy is still the only woman he wants, but falters mid-sentence.
The crowd reacts with shock and judgment, audible through gasps.
Tracy responds by saying she doesn't even know who he is and leaves.

memories.memory_text: I walked into the bar soaked and bruised, scanning for Jacob’s face, hoping he’d be at our usual table—but he wasn’t there.
grounded_facts: Cal entered the bar while physically injured and wet from rain.
Cal looked toward his usual table upon entering.
Cal expected or hoped to find Jacob there.
Jacob was absent from the bar when Cal arrived.

memories.memory_text: I'm sitting at the table Jacob used to haunt, and for the first time, I feel how heavy that kind of loneliness must've been—like no one sees you, like you're just waiting for a call that's never coming.
grounded_facts: Cal is sitting at Jacob's former table in the bar.
Cal appears suddenly alone, indicating a shift in his emotional state.
Cal observes another lonely man at the bar and picks up his cell phone, possibly reflecting on isolation.

memories.memory_text: I remember Jessica printing out a photo of herself—something bold, something meant for me—and the way she handled it like it was both dangerous and important.
grounded_facts: Jessica printed a photo from her camera.
The photo was intended for Cal, as indicated by the addressed envelope.

memories.memory_text: There was an envelope with my name on it, 'Cal Weaver,' and a heart—she wrote that, she stared at it, and I wasn’t even there but I can still see it in my head, like it was meant to be found.
grounded_facts: An envelope was addressed to 'Cal Weaver' with a heart drawn on it.
Jessica placed the photo inside the envelope and looked at it before hiding it.

memories.memory_text: I was outside in the dark, trimming and watering the yard like some ghost, when I saw Tracy bring out ice cream for the kids. They were laughing, all three of them together, and I just stood there, frozen, watching through the window like I didn’t belong.
grounded_facts: Cal is outside at night, tending to the backyard while observing his family inside.
Tracy brings out ice cream with three spoons, and the children join her, laughing.
Cal witnesses this family moment from the outside, feeling emotionally separated.

memories.memory_text: My phone rang and I flinched, trying to silence it fast—but then I saw her on the phone, pretending to be in the basement, faking the whole water heater thing. I could see her standing in the kitchen, and still I played along, telling her to push the button I’d labeled, like it meant something.
grounded_facts: Cal's phone rings, and he quickly silences it while watching Tracy inside.
Tracy pretends to be in the basement, calling for help with the water heater, though Cal sees she is in the kitchen.
Cal guides her through fake steps, referencing a red button he labeled, maintaining the deception.

memories.memory_text: I found an envelope in Jessica's drawer with my name on it, and when I opened it, whatever was inside nearly made me collapse.
grounded_facts: Cal's name is on an envelope found in Jessica's bottom drawer.
Claire discovered the envelope while putting away laundry.
The contents of the envelope caused a strong physical reaction in Claire.

memories.memory_text: I ran to him and saw the blood on his face, and even though I wanted to scream, I touched his forehead—my hand moved before I could stop it.
grounded_facts: Emily (referred to as Tracy) runs to Cal after he is injured.
Cal is on the curb with visible blood.
Tracy speaks angrily, saying 'Are you out of your mind!?'
Tracy touches Cal's bloody forehead in an affectionate manner.
Tracy then says 'Okay,' signaling a shift in tone.

memories.memory_text: I stood there holding the cologne Bernie brought, feeling the weight of it in my hands, not knowing what to say as he patted my shoulder and left without looking back.
grounded_facts: Bernie handed Cal a wrapped package containing cologne.
Bernie patted Cal on the shoulder before exiting the bar.
Cal remained seated and silent after Bernie left.

memories.scene_title: 19、EXT. THE WEAVER BACKYARD - DAY (ONE WEEK LATER)
memories.memory_text: I tried to tell Cal I was having a mid-life crisis, that I felt like I was becoming someone I never wanted to be, but he just kept talking about the sprinklers and fertilizer like none of it mattered.
grounded_facts: Tracy (Emily Weaver) mentions having a mid-life crisis and feeling like she's turning into someone she despised.
She brings up her outburst at Molly's t-ball game as evidence of this change.
Cal responds by giving lawn care instructions instead of engaging emotionally.

memories.scene_title: 19、EXT. THE WEAVER BACKYARD - DAY (ONE WEEK LATER)
memories.memory_text: I asked Cal when 'us' ended, and he looked at me and said it was because I slept with David Jacobowitz — I flinched, but I didn't deny it, because I knew it was true.
grounded_facts: Tracy asks Cal when they stopped being 'us'.
Cal responds by blaming her for sleeping with David Jacobowitz.
Tracy's face registers the impact of the accusation, and she nods in quiet acknowledgment.

memories.memory_text: I remember Cal saying I was wildly sexy and unbelievably cute, and that he missed me.
grounded_facts: Cal described Tracy as 'wildly sexy and unbelievably cute'.
Cal said 'I miss my wife' in the bar.

memories.memory_text: I saw the waitress hand him the bill after Jacob left with the last girl, and Cal just sat there—completely still—like he didn’t even know how to move.
grounded_facts: After Jacob left with multiple women, Cal was left alone at the table.
A waitress delivered the bill to Cal, indicating he was expected to pay.

memories.memory_text: I wasn't in the classroom, but I later learned Kate accused Cal in front of Tracy, and Tracy left upset.
grounded_facts: Emily Weaver is not present in the classroom scene.
Kate publicly accuses Cal of dishonesty and intimate acts in front of Tracy.
Tracy reacts with visible distress and leaves the room.

memories.memory_text: I kept walking and told Cal to leave me alone when he called after me.
grounded_facts: Emily (Tracy) is moving forward in the hallway, disengaging from Cal.
Cal shouts 'Tracy, wait!' while she is leaving.
She responds by saying 'Leave me alone, Cal!' in a public school setting.
There are onlookers present, including teachers, parents, and other adults.

memories.memory_text: I heard him say he’d been with eleven women, and that our private words were now something he’d thrown away—something cheap. I didn’t look back when I got in the car.
grounded_facts: The character hears Cal admit to sleeping with eleven women.
The character recognizes that Cal used their intimate phrase in front of others, violating its meaning.
The character leaves the scene by getting into her car and driving away.

memories.memory_text: I saw Cal's name on the screen and pressed ignore without saying a word, then looked up to see David smiling at me.
grounded_facts: Emily (Tracy) received a phone call from someone named Cal.
She saw the caller ID and deliberately chose to ignore the call.
David was sitting across from her at the restaurant table.
David smiled at her, and she responded with a weak smile.

memories.memory_text: I saw the flowers David gave Tracy sitting on the stoop with that note—'Just give me a chance, love David J'—but 'David J' was scratched out and 'Robbie' written over it. I shook my head and took them inside without saying anything.
grounded_facts: The flowers originally given by David Jacobowitz to Tracy are present on the stoop.
The note attached to the flowers has 'David J.' crossed out and replaced with 'Robbie'.
Emily observes the note, shakes her head, takes the flowers, and closes the door.
Emily does not speak or interact with anyone in the scene.

memories.memory_text: I stepped into the kitchen, closed the door, and called Cal, saying I was in the basement trying to fix the water heater—pilot light out, didn’t know how to relight it—while standing right there in the lit kitchen where he could see me.
grounded_facts: Tracy (Emily Weaver) made a phone call to Cal while inside the kitchen.
She claimed to be in the basement dealing with a water heater issue.
She performed physical actions like pantomiming pulling down a door and pushing a button as part of the ruse.
Cal could see her through the window during the call, making the lie visible.

memories.memory_text: After I hung up, Cal stayed outside and the sprinklers turned on, soaking him. I was in the kitchen. We didn’t speak. He didn’t come in.
grounded_facts: Tracy called Cal and pretended to be in the basement.
Cal knew she was not in the basement but played along with the call.
After the call ended, there was an awkward silence.
Cal remained outside in the yard when the sprinklers activated and got wet.
Tracy stayed inside the house, in the kitchen, after ending the call.

memories.memory_text: I saw Robbie standing by the front door in a tuxedo, that smug grin on his face, and I just sighed—another one of his stunts, right when I wanted to go inside and be done with the day.
grounded_facts: Robbie was standing outside the Weaver house in a tuxedo.
Robbie had a smug ("shit-eating") grin on his face.
Emily sighed upon seeing him, indicating resignation or irritation.

memories.memory_text: I felt the soft fabric of the scarf pressed against my eyes, then another layered over it, tied tightly from behind by Robbie’s hands.
grounded_facts: Robbie blindfolded Emily using multiple scarves.
The blindfolding occurred outdoors at the Weaver house.
Emily knelt down to allow Robbie to tie the blindfold.
Robbie tied the scarves around her eyes from behind.

memories.memory_text: I stood there blindfolded, the scarves tight over my eyes, and asked again if I could take them off—my voice was quiet, almost pleading—because I still didn’t know what any of it was for.
grounded_facts: Emily Weaver was present during the backyard scene with the blindfolded Tracy.
Tracy asked to remove her blindfold multiple times.
Emily did not speak or intervene, only observed.

memories.memory_text: When Hannah stepped out from the house, and then Jacob right behind her, I heard the shift in everyone’s breathing—something broke open, and I remember thinking, this is falling apart, before Tracy shouted, 'Who is she pointing at!?'
grounded_facts: Hannah and Jacob entered from inside the house.
Their appearance caused a visible freeze and shock among those present.
Tracy shouted 'Who is she pointing at!?' after seeing Hannah and Jacob.
Emily remained silent and did not react verbally.

memories.memory_text: I stood there and finally said it out loud — the house isn't his anymore, not after everything. My voice didn't shake, even though my hands did.
grounded_facts: Emily Weaver speaks the line: 'IT'S NOT YOUR HOUSE ANYMORE, CAL!' in the backyard scene.
The statement is directed at Cal during a confrontation after the police leave.
The scene is tense, with visible damage to the yard and emotional strain among the family.

memories.memory_text: I took down the picture of Cal and me from my office wall; the frame felt heavier than it should have.
grounded_facts: Emily Weaver (referred to as Tracy in the script) removes a picture of herself and Cal from her office.
The action occurs in her office one month after previous events, as part of a sequence showing emotional transitions.
The moment is framed as a deliberate, final act, signifying closure.

memories.memory_text: I accidentally made eye contact with Cal during the ceremony, and it felt so awkward—I could feel the tension between us, like we were both remembering everything that had happened.
grounded_facts: Emily is seated in the front row with Tracy, Molly, and Hannah.
Cal is seated many rows back in the audience.
Emily accidentally catches Cal's eye during the ceremony.
The moment of eye contact is described as awkward.

memories.memory_text: I saw Cal sitting far back, looking miserable, and when Jacob took the seat next to Hannah that might have been meant for him, I noticed how upset he became—even from a distance, his pain was visible.
grounded_facts: Cal is seated many rows back, looking miserable.
There is an empty seat between Tracy and Hannah.
Cal notices the empty seat and appears to wonder if it is for him.
Jacob arrives, kisses Hannah, and takes the seat.
Cal reacts with visible distress, described as looking nauseous.

memories.memory_text: I stood apart from the others, watching Cal and Tracy talk, and when she mentioned the ice cream, I saw the way their faces softened, like something small had finally held steady in all the mess.
grounded_facts: Emily Weaver is not mentioned in the scene and does not appear.
The emotional moment between Cal and Tracy about the ice cream occurs while Robbie is nearby, but Emily is not present.
Emily does not speak or interact with any character in this scene.

memories.memory_text: I caught her eye across the bar, raised my glass just enough to acknowledge her without saying a word — she’d been on my mind since the moment I walked in.
grounded_facts: Jacob Palmer is present in the bar and has been observing Liz for some time.
He makes eye contact with Liz and toasts her silently.
He is described as the 'smoothest, coolest son-of-a-bitch' and is aware of his effect on others.

memories.memory_text: I looked at Hannah and told her I found her incredibly sexy — not some line, but a fact — and that I’d been watching only her for two hours. Then I asked her straight up, no hesitation, if she found me attractive.
grounded_facts: Jacob directly states his attraction to Hannah, framing it as factual rather than performative.
He claims to have watched only her for the past two hours.
He asks her to answer quickly and without overthinking whether she finds him attractive.

memories.memory_text: After she hesitated, I ordered her a margarita — rocks, salt — like it was already decided, like I knew her. Just like that.
grounded_facts: Jacob orders a margarita with specific preparation (rocks, salt) for Hannah without her confirming the drink.
The order follows his proposition to buy her a drink and comes across as presumptuous and confident.
Hannah reacts with surprise ('Wow') before deciding to leave.

memories.memory_text: I was at my table in the bar with two women, and I was wearing my two-thousand-dollar suit.
grounded_facts: Jacob Palmer is at a bar with two women.
Jacob is wearing a two-thousand-dollar suit.

memories.memory_text: I was at my usual table, leaning in close, making the new woman laugh—just like that, another night, another connection sparking under my terms.
grounded_facts: Jacob is at his usual table in the bar.
Jacob is interacting with a new woman in a way that suggests romantic or sexual pursuit.
The interaction is confident and effective enough to be noticeable to an observer like Cal.

memories.memory_text: I zipped up, flushed, and told him not to start conversations while someone's pissing—then walked past him to wash my hands and left. I didn't look back, but I heard him mutter something under his breath after I was gone.
grounded_facts: Jacob was using the urinal next to Cal.
Cal attempted to make conversation about being surrounded by women and going through a divorce.
Jacob responded with 'Don't start conversations with people while they're pissing,' then washed his hands and exited.
Cal muttered 'So gay' after Jacob left.

memories.memory_text: I watched Cal fall apart after Bernie rejected him, saw the way he slumped over that bar with his sad little wallet photos, and I knew right then I could fix him — or at least remake him into something worth looking at.
grounded_facts: Jacob observed Cal being emotionally rejected by Bernie in the bar.
Jacob noticed Cal's disheveled appearance and pathetic behavior, including showing wallet photos to the bartender.
Jacob made a deliberate decision to intervene by whistling and calling Cal over.

memories.memory_text: I had Cal take off both of his New Balances, and when he handed them over, I didn't hesitate—I just turned and threw them over the railing. The look on his face was priceless.
grounded_facts: Jacob instructed Cal to remove both sneakers.
Cal complied and handed the shoes to Jacob.
Jacob threw the shoes over the railing in the food court.

memories.memory_text: I asked him if he was Steve Jobs—yelling it, really—because if he wasn't, there was no excuse for hiding behind those frumpy New Balances. It had to hit hard for him to feel it.
grounded_facts: Jacob directly questioned Cal about being Steve Jobs.
The question was repeated emphatically in a loud tone.
The comment was tied to justifying the rejection of Cal's footwear.

memories.memory_text: I told Cal I wrote that GQ article he remembered — he laughed, I laughed, but I could see he wasn’t sure whether to believe me or not.
grounded_facts: Jacob says 'You did. I wrote it.' in response to Cal referencing a GQ article.
Both Jacob and Cal chuckle after the revelation, with Cal appearing uncertain.

memories.memory_text: I picked out black dress shoes, slapped Cal's hand away from the argyle socks, and asked for his credit card. He gave it to me.
grounded_facts: Jacob selects a pair of black dress shoes.
Jacob slaps Cal's hand away from argyle socks.
Jacob asks Cal for his credit card.

memories.memory_text: I tossed Cal the eye cream and told him it gives x-ray vision, just to see the look on his face—then I pointed out how the skin around his eyes sags like Milton Berle's ballsack. He actually stopped and stared at himself in the mirror like he’d never seen it before.
grounded_facts: Jacob tosses Cal a tube of eye cream in a beauty supply store.
Jacob sarcastically says the cream gives x-ray vision, then mocks Cal's aging eye skin.
Cal examines his eyes in a mirror after Jacob's comment.

memories.memory_text: I overruled Cal at the salon, shut him down when he tried to give his own haircut instructions, and told Tiffany to go tight on the sides with texture on top—then I measured an inch off with my fingers like it was my own client sitting there.
grounded_facts: Jacob interrupts Cal’s haircut request at the salon.
Jacob gives specific haircut instructions to the stylist Tiffany.
Jacob uses his fingers to measure the length to cut from Cal's hair.
Tiffany acknowledges Jacob’s instructions and begins cutting.

memories.memory_text: I looked at Tiffany and asked her straight, 'You'd fuck him, right?'—and when she said yes, I could see Cal’s face fall. His personality’s a black hole, and pointing that out was the kindest cruelty.
grounded_facts: Jacob asked Tiffany if she would sleep with Cal after his transformation.
Tiffany responded affirmatively with 'Yeah, probably.'
Jacob told Cal that his personality was his weakest link.

memories.memory_text: I told Cal his wife cheated because he lost sight of what it took to keep her content as a man, a husband, and a lover. His lip started to quiver.
grounded_facts: Jacob told Cal that his wife cheated because he lost sight of what it took to keep her content.
Jacob specified that this failure was as a man, a husband, and a lover.
Cal's lower lip began to quiver after Jacob's statement.

memories.memory_text: I asked Cal how many women he'd been with, and when he said 'One,' I couldn't help but laugh—it was this perfect mix of shock and disbelief, like he was proud of it.
grounded_facts: Jacob directly asked Cal about his sexual history.
Cal responded with 'One,' referring to his wife.
Jacob reacted with audible laughter and visible surprise.

memories.memory_text: I used Cal's own words about his wife—'wildly sexy and incredibly cute'—on that newer girl, and it worked exactly as I knew it would; she said yes the moment I said it.
grounded_facts: Jacob repeated Cal's description of his wife to a new woman at the bar.
The phrase 'wildly sexy and incredibly cute' was originally spoken by Cal about Tracy.
The woman responded positively and agreed to leave with Jacob.

memories.memory_text: I looked at Cal and told him he was ready—really ready—to talk to a woman, to take one home, and I could see the doubt in his eyes, the way he shrank back like I’d asked him to jump off a cliff.
grounded_facts: Jacob says 'I think you're ready, Cal.'
Jacob observes Cal's appearance and behavior, noting he looks more comfortable and has adopted a more confident drink-ordering habit
Jacob initiates a conversation about Cal's readiness for real romantic engagement

memories.memory_text: I brought up Mr. Miagi and Karate Kid, watching Cal’s face go from confused to horrified, and I knew he finally understood—this wasn’t just watching me pick up women, it was training, every move part of a pattern he’d learned without realizing it.
grounded_facts: Jacob references the Karate Kid and Mr. Miagi's training method
Jacob draws a parallel between his mentorship and the film's hidden skill-building
Cal reacts with discomfort and recognition

memories.memory_text: I approached a table of women and smiled. They looked up, and I introduced myself.
grounded_facts: Jacob approached a group of women at the bar.
Jacob smiled at the women.
The women looked up in response.

memories.memory_text: Hannah burst in, soaked and wild-eyed, grabbed me and kissed me hard—like she was angry. Then she asked if I still wanted to take her home, and I said yes. I didn’t hesitate.
grounded_facts: Hannah entered the bar suddenly and approached Jacob.
Hannah kissed Jacob passionately, then asked him three direct questions, including whether he still wanted to take her home.
Jacob answered 'yes' to all three questions and left the bar with her.

memories.memory_text: I remember laughing when she admitted she was nervous, and saying 'You're adorable' before she snapped at me — it caught me off guard how raw and real she suddenly seemed.
grounded_facts: Hannah admitted she was nervous.
Jacob laughed and called her adorable.
Hannah reacted strongly, rejecting being called 'adorable'.

memories.memory_text: She told me to take off my shirt, her voice sharp and insistent, and I remember standing up, unbuttoning it slowly, feeling her eyes on me as she poked my abs and said 'Holy crap.'
grounded_facts: Hannah demanded Jacob take off his shirt.
Jacob stood, unbuttoned, and removed his shirt.
Hannah reacted physically and verbally to his physique.

memories.memory_text: I was on top of her, about to kiss her again, and she asked if I had one of those ridiculous massage chairs—then started laughing hysterically when I admitted I did. Her laughter doubled her over, and I just started laughing too, really laughing, and in that moment I realized I actually liked her.
grounded_facts: Hannah asked Jacob if he had a massage chair.
Jacob initially lied but then admitted he owned one.
Hannah laughed hysterically at the admission.
Jacob began laughing genuinely and realized he liked her during the shared laughter.

memories.memory_text: She asked what my mom was like, and I said 'icy... I guess? Not cruel, but cold. Very cold.' Then she asked about my father, and I told her he died long ago and left us money—that’s how I can afford things like this pillow. I could feel something shift, like I was letting someone see me for the first time.
grounded_facts: Hannah asked Jacob about his mother.
Jacob described his mother as emotionally cold.
Hannah asked about his father.
Jacob revealed his father had died and left the family money.
Jacob connected his possessions to the inheritance from his father.

memories.memory_text: I saw Hannah looking at my pancakes, so I just switched plates with her without saying a word. She smiled, and I smiled back—I couldn’t help it.
grounded_facts: Jacob notices Hannah looking longingly at his pancakes.
Jacob switches plates with Hannah.
Hannah smiles in response, and Jacob smiles back.

memories.memory_text: I felt her hand in mine, warm and soft, but all I could see was how trapped I was—my own breath turning shallow, my skin crawling like I couldn’t get away fast enough.
grounded_facts: Jacob is holding hands with Hannah in a movie theatre.
Jacob is visibly terrified while holding her hand.
He is not focused on the movie but on his own emotional reaction.

memories.memory_text: I was just looking at her, really looking—she was so peaceful asleep, and for a second, everything felt quiet and right. Then I realized I was doing it again, staring like some lovesick idiot.
grounded_facts: Jacob stares at Hannah while she sleeps.
He catches himself mid-gaze, indicating self-awareness.

memories.memory_text: I couldn't stop moving—pacing, drinking, pulling at my hair—like if I didn't do something physical, the feeling would tear me apart. I kept looking back at her, helpless, like I was losing control of everything.
grounded_facts: Jacob paces, drinks heavily, pulls at his hair, and hyperventilates in a series of comedic jump-cuts.
He alternates between agitated behavior and staring at Hannah.

memories.memory_text: I tucked her in, kissed her forehead, and just... let it happen — I gave in, finally, to loving her.
grounded_facts: Jacob Palmer tucks Hannah under a blanket.
Jacob Palmer kisses Hannah's forehead.
Jacob gives in to falling in love with Hannah.

memories.memory_text: I told Cal I met a woman and that I've been spending a lot of time with her — just one woman — and I could hear how hard he was laughing, like he didn't believe me.
grounded_facts: Jacob told Cal he met a woman and has been spending a lot of time with her.
Jacob emphasized it was one woman, indicating exclusivity.
Cal responded with disbelief and laughter.

memories.memory_text: Hannah came out of the wine store, hopped over the car, and I couldn't stop myself from smiling — I remember feeling this rush, just seeing her.
grounded_facts: Hannah exited the wine store and moved toward Jacob's car.
Jacob smiled upon seeing her, visibly affected by her presence.
His reaction was involuntary and strong, indicating deep emotional engagement.

memories.memory_text: I walked in behind Hannah, holding a bottle of wine and smiling, feeling good—then the room froze and Cal stared at me like he’d seen a ghost.
grounded_facts: Jacob entered the backyard behind Hannah, holding a bottle of wine.
Jacob smiled upon entry, before realizing Cal was present.
Cal reacted with shock and disbelief upon seeing Jacob.

memories.memory_text: When Hannah introduced me as her boyfriend, I saw Cal’s face twist—he said he’d murder me, and I knew this wasn’t going to end quietly.
grounded_facts: Hannah introduced Jacob as her boyfriend to Cal.
Cal responded with immediate hostility, saying 'End it, now' and then 'I'm going to murder you.'
Jacob remained defiant, refusing to end the relationship.

memories.memory_text: I walked into the graduation and saw Hannah sitting in the front row—my place felt waiting, so I kissed her cheek and slid into the empty seat between her and Tracy.
grounded_facts: Jacob arrives at the graduation ceremony.
He kisses Hannah.
He takes the seat between Tracy and Hannah that was previously empty.

memories.memory_text: As I settled in, I noticed Cal way in the back—our eyes didn’t meet, but I could feel his stare, heavy and sick, like he’d been punched and couldn’t breathe.
grounded_facts: Cal is seated many rows back among random spectators.
Cal looks nauseous after Jacob takes the seat.
There is no direct interaction between Jacob and Cal, but Cal's reaction is visible.

memories.memory_text: Cal told me he ordered a Colt .45 online and would shoot me in the face if I hurt Hannah, and when he told me to shut up, I did — then he said he liked that.
grounded_facts: Cal threatened Jacob with a gun if he hurt Hannah.
Cal told Jacob to shut up, and Jacob complied.
Cal responded positively, saying 'Wow. I like that.'

memories.memory_text: After the threat, Cal noticed my shirt and asked if it was new, and I joked that Hannah took me to the Gap but I actually bought it at Nordstrom's.
grounded_facts: Cal asked if Jacob was wearing a new shirt.
Jacob responded with a humorous lie about the Gap, then corrected himself, saying he bought it at Nordstrom's.
